acceptanceCriteria: Exactly six Main KPIs render as one hero plus five cards; Net Revenue and 30 days are defaults
SPS, ROAS, and CSAT remain visible, unavailable, non-selectable, and never show fake zero/fixture data
Available KPI selection performs a literal hero/card swap and updates browser history
Net Revenue, Inventory Turnover, and Fulfillment Accuracy Rate use documented mock charts and values
Range updates hero and available previews transactionally; comparison affects hero only
Provenance, freshness, related Decision deep links, invalid deep links, loading, partial, and error states meet the screen contract

redGreenRefactorEvidence.acceptanceCriterion: Six Main KPI catalog and defaults

redGreenRefactorEvidence.green: Range-scoped snapshots with provenance and workflow deep links
redGreenRefactorEvidence.refactor: Shared time-series builders for preview/hero parity

redGreenRefactorEvidence.green: Hero/card swap, range chips, comparison toggle, invalid deep link, error retry
redGreenRefactorEvidence.refactor: Split charts into analytics-charts.tsx; URL/state sync only on routeMetricKey change

implementationSummary: Implemented six-KPI Analytics dashboard in apps/demo with hero/card literal swap, range and comparison controls, unavailable KPI popovers, mock fixture data for three available KPIs, and comprehensive vitest coverage mapped to P2-6 acceptance criteria.
assumptions: Manual Refresh navigation to /decisions unmounts Analytics before URL prop can re-sync mutable analyticsMetric
Fulfillment Accuracy gauge uses @juli/ui ProgressBar meter per Phase 2.6 demo scope
risks: Parallel #405 may append demo-state keys; merge should preserve analyticsComparisonEnabled
